news.title: Tampa employee of Cuban origin wasn’t subjected to unlawful harassment, court rules
news.summary: The alleged incidents were sporadic, contained no racial or ethnic slurs, and for the most part, made no express reference to Hispanics.

news.title: Title VII plaintiffs don’t need to mitigate emotional distress damages, 5th Circuit says
news.summary: No such requirement exists in the law’s statute, the court held, rejecting an argument advanced by SkyWest Airlines in a former employee’s harassment lawsuit.

news.title: AI skills now listed in 73% of tech job postings
news.summary: Highly regulated industries working to shape their AI implementation plans outpace other industries in the search for talent, according to Dice.

news.title: The economy is shutting young adults out of career-entry jobs, analysis finds
news.summary: Artificial intelligence matters, but in a “narrow, early and age-specific way,” researchers at the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis said.

news.title: DC Water to pay roughly $217,000 to settle claim it replaced HR worker with younger employee
news.summary: The District of Columbia Water and Sewer Authority must also enhance its nondiscrimination policies and provide advanced antidiscrimination training.

news.title: This week in 5 numbers: Nearly 4 in 10 workers don’t trust HR to help in toxic situations
news.summary: Here’s a roundup of numbers from the last week — including how many workers were targeted in job scam attempts.

news.title: Men outearn women by more than $12K annually, data shows
news.summary: “As more women joined the full-time workforce, the per-worker difference compounded into a much larger estimated overall gap,” a career expert said.

news.title: On its 60th birthday, SHRM’s nonprofit arm pushes for better caregiver support
news.summary: Employers can’t reliably address working caregivers’ needs through policies and employee benefits alone, said Wendi Safstrom, president of the SHRM Foundation.

news.title: WorkSmart settles EEOC claim it failed to hire, refer women per a client request
news.summary: Staffing agencies have repeatedly drawn the agency’s attention for allegedly discriminating based on protected characteristics to satisfy the demands of a client.

news.title: Nearly 7 in 10 workers say they didn’t get a raise in the past 6 months
news.summary: Increased transparency about pay and workforce decisions can improve the perception gap between employers and employees, Morgan McKinley found.
